landing.heroDescription: Transitivity 2.0 is the platform that combines transition state theory, kinetic fitting, molecular dynamics, and machine learning potentials in a single environment.
landing.aboutP1: Transitivity 2.0 is the web evolution of the Transitivity code developed at the University of Brasilia. It brings rate constant calculations, kinetic model fitting, molecular dynamics input generation, and ML predictions into a unified SaaS platform.
landing.aboutP2: Designed for researchers who need professional-grade computational chemistry tools without managing complex local installations. Upload your files, compute, analyze.
landing.featRateTitle: Transition State Theory with tunneling corrections
landing.featRateDesc: Calculate rate constants using TST with five tunneling correction methods and three solvent models. Upload Gaussian, ORCA, or GAMESS output files.
landing.featRateB3: Automatic parsing of quantum chemistry outputs via cclib
landing.featRateB4: Arrhenius and Transitivity plots with CSV export
landing.featFitTitle: Fit kinetic models with Generalized Simulated Annealing
landing.featFitDesc: Fit experimental rate constant data to six kinetic models using a global optimization algorithm that avoids local minima.
landing.featFitB1: Arrhenius, Aquilanti-Mundim, NTS, VFT, ASCC, SATO
landing.featFitB3: Multi-model comparison with R² and chi-square
landing.featMlTitle: Neural network potentials for molecular predictions
landing.featMlDesc: Run single-point energy calculations and geometry optimizations using state-of-the-art machine learning potentials.
landing.featMlB1: ANI-2x for organic molecules (H, C, N, O, F, S, Cl)
landing.howSubtitle: Quick setup, intuitive workflow, immediate results.
landing.step1Desc: Upload your quantum chemistry output files or enter experimental data. We parse Gaussian, ORCA, GAMESS and more.
landing.step2Desc: Run rate constant calculations, fit kinetic models, or predict molecular properties with neural network potentials.
landing.step3Desc: Visualize results with interactive plots, compare models, and export publication-ready data in CSV format.
landing.demoSubtitle: Start computing in minutes, not days.
landing.pricingSubtitle: Start free. No credit card, no surprises.
landing.planFreeDesc: For students and small research groups

rateConstant.title: Rate Constants
rateConstant.description: Calculate TST rate constants with tunneling corrections and solvent effects.
rateConstant.workspaceDesc: Upload species, configure every request field, and inspect the rate-constant output in one workspace.
rateConstant.noReactions: No reactions yet. Create one to get started.
rateConstant.speciesDescription: Upload Gaussian/ORCA/GAMESS output files for each species. The files will be parsed automatically.
rateConstant.needReactant: At least one reactant is required.
rateConstant.customTempsHint: Enter temperatures separated by commas or spaces
rateConstant.temperaturesHint: Separate values with spaces, commas, or new lines. These numbers will be sent directly as temperatures.
rateConstant.temperaturesRequired: Add at least one valid temperature before running the calculation.
rateConstant.notComputed: This reaction has not been computed yet.
rateConstant.arrheniusPlotDesc: Compare the computed methods on the Arrhenius view using the live response returned by the backend.
rateConstant.workspaceReactionDesc: Keep the core reaction settings visible while you prepare the files and the endpoint options.
rateConstant.endpointSpeciesDesc: Upload the reactants, transition state, and products that will feed the rate-constant endpoint.
rateConstant.endpointOptionsDesc: Edit the temperature list, tunneling corrections, and solvent parameters without leaving the page.
rateConstant.resultsSummaryDesc: Barrier and crossover values returned by the backend appear here as soon as the calculation finishes.
rateConstant.rateConstantsResponseDesc: Inspect the rate constants for each method using the exact arrays returned by the endpoint.
rateConstant.speciesSnapshotDesc: Review the parsed species properties alongside the results.
rateConstant.noEndpointResult: Run the calculation to inspect the response and plots without leaving this screen.
rateConstant.noPlotData: The Arrhenius chart will appear here after a successful calculation.
rateConstant.noSpeciesLoaded: No species loaded yet. Upload a file and assign it to a reaction role.

fitting.dataInputDescription: Enter or upload experimental rate constant data (T vs k). You can type values, paste from a spreadsheet, or upload a file.
fitting.pasteData: Tip: paste tab-separated data from a spreadsheet
fitting.minPoints: At least 3 valid data points are required

fittingWizard.title: Configure Fitting
fittingWizard.subtitle: Set up and run kinetic model fitting on your experimental data.
fittingWizard.hubDesc: Fit kinetic models to experimental rate constant data using Generalized Simulated Annealing.
fittingWizard.workspaceDesc: Run a single fitting job using the exact request fields and response structure exposed by the endpoint.
fittingWizard.dataInputDesc: Enter or upload experimental rate constant data.
fittingWizard.endpointDataDesc: Fill the temperature and rate constant pairs that will be sent as temperatures and rate_constants.
fittingWizard.theorySetupDesc: Choose one theory, define the initial parameters, and lock any values you do not want the optimizer to change.
fittingWizard.gsaFieldsDesc: These fields map directly to the gsa object accepted by the endpoint.
fittingWizard.endpointOutputDesc: The cards, chart, and table below are derived from the response returned by /fit.
fittingWizard.noEndpointResult: Run the fitting to inspect the response payload and the generated Arrhenius curve.
fittingWizard.payloadPreviewDesc: This is the JSON body that will be posted to the fitting endpoint.
fittingWizard.endpointNotesDesc: Reference notes for the input and output shape used in this screen.
fittingWizard.notesTheoryDesc: The endpoint expects one theory at a time, so the workspace now executes one fit per request.
fittingWizard.notesParametersDesc: The number and labels of initial_params and lock entries change according to the selected theory.
fittingWizard.notesOutputDesc: The output section is built from theory, chi_square, parameters, and curve exactly as returned by the service.
fittingWizard.fittingMinPoints: Add at least 2 valid data points before running the fit.
fittingWizard.configureFittingDesc: Select datasets, models, and parameters to run fitting.
fittingWizard.viewResultsDesc: Compare fitted models and download results.
fittingWizard.selectDatasetDesc: Choose an experimental dataset to fit.
fittingWizard.selectModelsDesc: Choose one or more kinetic models to fit to the data.
fittingWizard.gsaParametersDesc: Optionally customize Generalized Simulated Annealing parameters.
fittingWizard.reviewRunDesc: Review your configuration and start the fitting.
fittingWizard.noDatasets: No experimental datasets found. Please add data first.
fittingWizard.noResults: No fitting results found. Run a fitting first.

md.cellDescription: Define the simulation cell lattice parameters.

ml.modelSelectDesc: Choose a neural network potential model.
ml.modelANI2xDesc: General-purpose potential for H, C, N, O, F, S, Cl. Fast and accurate for organic molecules.
ml.modelMACEDesc: Equivariant message-passing potential. High accuracy across the periodic table.
ml.modelAIQM1Desc: AI-enhanced quantum mechanical method. Near coupled-cluster accuracy for small molecules.
ml.taskSelectDesc: Choose what type of calculation to perform.
ml.taskEnergyDesc: Compute the energy and forces for the current geometry.
ml.taskOptimizeDesc: Find the minimum energy geometry by optimizing atom positions.
ml.reviewDesc: Review your settings and run the prediction.
ml.computing: Computing... This may take a few moments.

course.heroSubtitle: From ML fundamentals to graph neural networks
course.heroDescription: A hands-on course covering machine learning, deep learning, graph neural networks, and convolutional neural networks applied to chemistry problems. Interactive notebooks in Google Colab.
course.mod1Desc: Foundations of machine learning applied to chemical problems. Covers data preprocessing, feature engineering for molecular descriptors, and classical ML algorithms such as random forests and SVMs for molecular property prediction.
course.mod1Note1: Scikit-learn pipelines for molecular data: scaling, splitting, and cross-validation.
course.mod1Note2: Molecular descriptors and fingerprints (RDKit) as input features for ML models.
course.mod1Note3: Comparison of linear regression, random forests, and gradient boosting for property prediction.
course.mod2Desc: Introduction to neural networks and deep learning architectures. Learn about feedforward networks, activation functions, backpropagation, and training strategies specifically designed for chemical datasets.
course.mod2Note1: Building neural networks in PyTorch: layers, activations, and loss functions.
course.mod2Note2: Regularization techniques: dropout, batch normalization, and early stopping.
course.mod2Note3: Hyperparameter tuning and learning rate scheduling for molecular property models.
course.mod3Desc: Molecular representations as graphs and message-passing neural networks. Explore how GNNs learn molecular features directly from atomic connectivity for property prediction and virtual screening.
course.mod3Note1: Molecules as graphs: atoms as nodes, bonds as edges, with feature vectors.
course.mod3Note2: Message-passing framework: aggregation, update, and readout operations.
course.mod3Note3: Hands-on with PyTorch Geometric for molecular property prediction tasks.
course.mod4Desc: Convolutional neural networks for molecular image representations and fingerprint-based learning. Explore how spatial patterns in molecular data can be captured with CNN architectures.
course.mod4Note1: Representing molecules as 2D images and Coulomb matrices for CNN input.
course.mod4Note2: Convolutional layers, pooling, and feature maps applied to chemical structures.
course.mod4Note3: Transfer learning from pretrained vision models for molecular classification.
course.landingCtaDesc: Explore our course on AI applied to chemistry with interactive Colab notebooks.
